frameworks.name: intrinsic gain modulation
frameworks.one_line: single-cell input-output gain sets the timescale of cortical circuit dynamics

frameworks.name: cell-type-specific top-down perturbation theory
frameworks.one_line: classifying how cell-type-targeted projections reshape stimulus processing speed

frameworks.name: recurrent cortical circuit dynamics
frameworks.one_line: circuit-level computations underlying state-dependent information processing

frameworks.name: neuromodulation and corticocortical top-down control
frameworks.one_line: candidate pathways that implement state- and context-dependent modulation

core_question: How do cell-type-specific top-down perturbations control the speed of cortical stimulus processing?
key_assumptions: behavioral flexibility reflects state-dependent modulation early in the cortical hierarchy
top-down projections act on identified cortical cell types
circuit timescale is governed by intrinsic single-cell gain
manipulations: cell-type-specific top-down perturbations in a theoretical circuit model
behavioral state (locomotion vs stationary) in freely running mice
key_findings: perturbation effects on processing speed are predicted by intrinsic gain modulation
increased input variance can counterintuitively improve performance
locomotion decreases single-cell intrinsic gain in visual cortex
lower intrinsic gain accelerates visual stimulus processing during locomotion
interpretations: intrinsic gain is a unifying mechanism linking top-down perturbations to processing-speed changes
state-dependent cortical acceleration arises from gain-controlled circuit timescales
